title: stylidium bicolor
intro: Stylidium bicolor, commonly known as the 'Rice Flower', is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Stylidiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Stylidium bicolor include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Stylidium bicolor can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by mixing the plant's extracts with other herbs to create a medicinal syrup.
